principles.addressStorage: Store breadcrumb nodes, format rules, aliases, quality scores, and source metadata in GitHub; do not store raw personal addresses.
principles.agidStorage: Store stable AGID ids, parent ids, repository ownership, validity intervals, and conformance fixtures; keep heavy geometry external.
principles.reconstruction: Restore an address by walking parent ids from unit/building/locality to country, then render through the country or territory format rule.
principles.heavyData: Move building polygons, high-resolution coordinates, search indexes, tiles, caches, and OSM/Overture extracts to content-addressed external packs.

CN.reason: Large population, province-scale source cadence, script/romanization complexity, megacity churn.
CN.continuityRule: Country index owns national codes, parent chains, and neutral region identifiers; province, SAR, Taiwan-region, and megacity repos own child nodes with stable parent ids without expressing a political position.

IN.reason: Very large population, multilingual address behavior, state-scale postal and locality variation.
IN.continuityRule: Country repo owns PIN, state/union-territory anchors, and language/transliteration policy; state and metro repos own locality, street, building, dialect-derived aliases, and city-specific delivery evidence.

ID.reason: Archipelagic geography, province-scale administration, many no-postal or weak-address edge cases.
ID.continuityRule: Country repo owns province/regency anchors and archipelago-wide postcode policy; province, island, Papua-region, and metro repos own locality, road, building, port, and external geo-pack evidence.

JP.reason: Dense addressing, municipality churn, high-quality GSI/Japan Post evidence, and megacity-scale building aliases.
JP.continuityRule: Country repo owns JIS, prefecture, Japan Post, and GSI anchors; prefecture repos own municipality/locality/block references, while city and Tokyo-23 repos own high-density building, alias, and romanization nodes.

PK.reason: Large population, province-scale administration, Urdu/English address variation, and postal-data quality differences.
PK.continuityRule: Country repo owns province and postcode rules; province/metro repos own locality aliases and delivery evidence.

BD.reason: Very high population density, division/district hierarchy, Bengali/English rendering, and dense urban growth.
BD.continuityRule: Country repo owns division, district, and postcode anchors; division and metro repos own ward, road, building, Bengali-English rendering, and dense urban delivery evidence.

IR.reason: Large population, province-scale administration, Persian romanization, and postal-code validation needs.
IR.continuityRule: Country repo owns national postal and province anchors; province/city repos own city, district, street, Persian romanization, and transliteration variants.

TR.reason: Large transcontinental country, province-scale addresses, Istanbul density, Turkish romanization, and Europe/Asia bridge routing.
TR.continuityRule: Country repo owns province and postal anchors across Europe/Asia; province and metro repos own district, mahalle, street, building, Turkish romanization, and route evidence.

SA.reason: Large area, Arabic/English delivery formats, national address system, and city-scale logistics growth.
SA.continuityRule: Country repo owns national address and region anchors; region and city repos own district, street, building, Arabic-English rendering, and delivery evidence.

PH.reason: Large archipelago, barangay-level addressing, English/Filipino forms, and metro-scale delivery complexity.
PH.continuityRule: Country repo owns region, province, Metro Manila, and barangay anchors; province, metro, and city repos own road, subdivision, building, postal, English/Filipino, and island delivery evidence.

VN.reason: Large population, province/city hierarchy, Vietnamese diacritics, and fast urban address changes.
VN.continuityRule: Country repo owns province, municipality, district, ward, and diacritic anchors; province/metro repos own road, house-number, alley, building, and romanization evidence.

TH.reason: Large population, province/district/subdistrict hierarchy, Thai-English transliteration, and Bangkok density.
TH.continuityRule: Country repo owns province and postcode anchors; province, metro, and tourism-city repos own district, tambon, road, soi, Thai-English transliteration, and delivery/tourism evidence.

MY.reason: Federal states, multilingual Malay/English/Chinese/Tamil address forms, and strong urban logistics corridors.
MY.continuityRule: Country repo owns state, federal-territory, and postcode anchors; state/metro repos own locality, road, building, and Malay/English/Chinese/Tamil aliases.

KR.reason: Dense road-name addressing, building-number systems, Korean romanization, and Seoul metro scale.
KR.continuityRule: Country repo owns road-name, building-number, postcode, and Revised Romanization rules; province/metro repos own building, dong, road, and romanization aliases.

US.reason: Federal scale, state-level addressing, USPS/territory behavior, heavy building and route data.
US.continuityRule: Country repo owns national schema, USPS ZIP rules, territories, and parent ids; state, territory, D.C., and megacity repos own local admin, street, building, and high-frequency delivery nodes.

BR.reason: Continental scale, state-scale administration, major metro churn, CEP-heavy address validation.
BR.continuityRule: Country repo owns CEP and state anchors; state/metro repos keep breadcrumb children continuous.

RU.reason: Very large area, federal subjects, multilingual/transliteration needs, sparse-region delivery behavior.
RU.continuityRule: Country repo owns federal hierarchy and neutral claim-aware anchors; subject repos own settlement/street nodes and aliases, while city repos own dense address updates without expressing a political position.

DE.reason: Dense addressing, federal states, strong open data, and high update quality expectations.
DE.continuityRule: Country repo owns national rules and postcode anchors; Land repos own municipality/street/building references, while city/metro repos own high-density alias and update streams.

GB.reason: UK addressing, Crown Dependencies, Overseas Territories, and London-specific density.
GB.continuityRule: Country repo owns UK-wide address rules and postcode anchors; nation, England-region, and major-city repos preserve parent ids. Crown Dependencies and Overseas Territories stay as independent country/territory repos.

CA.reason: Large area, province/territory addressing, bilingual English/French needs, indigenous/place-name variation, and strong open data sources.
CA.continuityRule: Country repo owns province/territory and postal-code anchors; child repos own municipality, street, building, and bilingual aliases.

MX.reason: Large population, state-level administration, Spanish locality variants, postal-code validation, and Mexico City density.
MX.continuityRule: Country repo owns state and codigo postal anchors; state/metro repos own colonia, municipality, street, and Spanish variants.

AR.reason: Large country, province-scale administration, CPA/postal-code handling, Buenos Aires density, and Spanish locality variation.
AR.continuityRule: Country repo owns province and postal anchors; child repos own locality, street, building, and delivery-zone evidence.

CO.reason: Large population, department/municipality hierarchy, strong national geo data, and metro-scale addressing.
CO.continuityRule: Country repo owns department and municipality anchors; child repos own barrio, street, building, and source evidence.

PE.reason: Large area, department/province/district hierarchy, Andean/rural address variation, and Lima density.
PE.continuityRule: Country repo owns department and district anchors; child repos own locality, route, community, and postal/geo evidence.

FR.reason: Dense addressing, overseas departments/collectivities, INSEE/postcode structure, and Paris-scale urban detail.
FR.continuityRule: Country repo owns INSEE/postcode and overseas anchors; region/territory repos own commune, street, building, and alias data.

IT.reason: Large population, region/province/commune hierarchy, dense historic cities, and Italian address formatting.
IT.continuityRule: Country repo owns region/province/CAP anchors; region repos own commune/street/building references, while city repos own dense historic aliases and high-frequency urban updates.

ES.reason: Autonomous communities, co-official languages, overseas/island regions, and dense metro data.
ES.continuityRule: Country repo owns province/postcode anchors; autonomous-community and city repos own municipality, street, building, island, and co-official language aliases.

PL.reason: Large EU population, voivodeship hierarchy, dense municipality data, and strong postal/address validation needs.
PL.continuityRule: Country repo owns voivodeship and postal anchors; voivodeship repos own gmina, locality, street, and building evidence, while city/metro repos own high-density urban updates.

RO.reason: Medium-size country with 41 counties plus Bucharest, detailed county administration, and strong city-level address density.
RO.continuityRule: Country repo owns county, Bucharest, and postal anchors; county repos own locality and street references, while city repos own high-density urban updates.

SE.reason: County-based administration, strong postcode/address data, multilingual northern place-name needs, and dense Stockholm/Gothenburg/Malmo updates.
SE.continuityRule: Country repo owns county and postcode anchors; county repos own municipality/locality/street references, while city repos own high-density urban and alias updates.

UA.reason: Large country, oblast/raion hierarchy, Cyrillic/romanization needs, wartime/disputed-region handling, and frequent data changes.
UA.continuityRule: Country repo owns oblast and neutral claim-aware anchors; oblast/city repos own settlement, street, building, romanization, and disputed-status evidence without expressing a political position.

NG.reason: Very large population, state/LGA hierarchy, mixed formal and informal addressing, and Lagos/Abuja logistics density.
NG.continuityRule: Country repo owns state/LGA anchors; child repos own locality, street, landmark, delivery-zone, and confidence evidence.

ET.reason: Large population, regional-state hierarchy, Amharic/English rendering, and fast urban/rural address variation.
ET.continuityRule: Country repo owns region and woreda anchors; child repos own locality, kebele, road, landmark, and transliteration evidence.

ZA.reason: Province-scale administration, multilingual place names, strong urban datasets, and township/rural delivery variation.
ZA.continuityRule: Country repo owns province and postcode anchors; child repos own municipality, suburb, street, building, and multilingual aliases.

EG.reason: Large population, governorate hierarchy, Arabic/English rendering, Cairo/Alexandria density, and postal-source variation.
EG.continuityRule: Country repo owns governorate and postal anchors; child repos own district, street, building, and Arabic romanization.

KE.reason: County-based administration, English/Swahili address use, Nairobi density, and rural delivery differences.
KE.continuityRule: Country repo owns county and postal anchors; child repos own subcounty, ward, road, estate, and delivery evidence.

TZ.reason: Large area and population, region/district hierarchy, Swahili/English rendering, and island/mainland differences.
TZ.continuityRule: Country repo owns region/district anchors; child repos own ward, street, village, island, and postal/geo evidence.

DZ.reason: Large area, wilaya/commune hierarchy, Arabic/French rendering, and sparse-region address variation.
DZ.continuityRule: Country repo owns wilaya and postcode anchors; child repos own commune, street, building, and Arabic/French aliases.

CD.reason: Very large area, province-scale administration, weak-address regions, and humanitarian/logistics use cases.
CD.continuityRule: Country repo owns province anchors; child repos own locality, route, facility, natural-feature, and manual-review evidence.

AU.reason: Continental scale, state/territory administration, remote communities, and rich open geo data.
AU.continuityRule: Country repo owns state/territory and postcode anchors; child repos own locality, road, parcel/building, and remote evidence. Australian external territories are independent single territory packs.

CL.reason: Long north-south geography, region/commune hierarchy, Andes/coastal delivery constraints, and strong national geo data.
CL.continuityRule: Country repo owns region, commune, postcode, and Antarctic-adjacent anchors; child repos own locality, street, coastal/mountain constraints, and Spanish aliases.

NL.reason: Province/municipality hierarchy, dense postcode data, Dutch/Frisian names, and Caribbean constituent/special municipalities.
NL.continuityRule: Country repo owns province/municipality, postcode, Dutch/Frisian/Caribbean anchors, and Kingdom-linked overseas territory continuity. Child repos own street, house-number, addition, building, island evidence, and territory-specific rendering without duplicating independent territory repos.

CH.reason: Canton/commune hierarchy, German/French/Italian/Romansh rendering, dense postcodes, and alpine delivery constraints.
CH.continuityRule: Country repo owns canton, commune, postcode, and four-language anchors; child repos own street, house-number, building, alpine locality, and language rendering.

AT.reason: State/municipality hierarchy, German addressing, Vienna density, and alpine/valley locality variation.
AT.continuityRule: Country repo owns state, municipality, postcode, and German anchors; child repos own locality, street, house-number, alpine route, and building evidence.

PT.reason: District/autonomous-region hierarchy, Portuguese addressing, island territories, and Lisbon/Porto density.
PT.continuityRule: Country repo owns district, autonomous-region, postcode, and Portuguese anchors; child repos own municipality, parish, street, building, and island evidence.

GR.reason: Region/regional-unit hierarchy, Greek/Latin transliteration, islands, Athens density, and postal validation needs.
GR.continuityRule: Country repo owns region, municipality, postcode, Greek, and transliteration anchors; child repos own street, number, building, island, and Orthodox/autonomous-area evidence.
